mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction starts combined with expanding mortgage debt outstanding indicate synchronized housing market strength and credit availability, which historically precedes broad economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, an uptick in the industrial production index reflects rebounding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with subsequent equity returns during identified recovery periods, or the forward return distribution shows no skew toward positive outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0%, it confirms an economic slowdown that compresses earnings expectations and forces equity de-rating, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite GDP growth below the 1.0% threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which historically sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor indicators produce a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and forces monetary policy tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during sustained CPI uptrends, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a housing market correction signals synchronized supply-side contraction and weakening aggregate demand, which historically triggers equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint supply shock fails to predict negative equity returns, or forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite the industrial output decline.
